doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, Dr. Lee. I've been having these coughing fits and wheezing episodes, usually once or twice a week. They sometimes make it hard to breathe, especially when I’m playing soccer or when it’s cold outside.

doctor_turn: How long do these coughing fits and wheezing episodes usually last?
patient_turn: They usually last about 30 to 60 minutes. Sometimes they even wake me up at night, which is really frustrating.

doctor_turn: Can you tell me where you feel the coughing or wheezing the most?
patient_turn: It feels like it's mostly in my chest and throat when I cough or wheeze. It’s kind of tight and makes it hard to breathe sometimes.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the coughing and wheezing episodes starting?
patient_turn: Oh, I think it started a couple of months ago, but I’m pretty sure it was last week instead.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the coughing and wheezing episodes?
patient_turn: I first noticed the coughing and wheezing episodes a few months ago. They started happening more often, especially when I was playing soccer or when it was cold outside.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any chest pain during your coughing fits?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any chest pain during my coughing fits. It’s mostly just the coughing and wheezing.

doctor_turn: Have you had any fevers or illnesses recently?
patient_turn: I had a fever a few months ago, but I haven’t had any fevers or illnesses recently. Just these coughing fits and wheezing episodes.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any headaches or feelings of your heart racing during these episodes?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t noticed any headaches or my heart racing during the episodes. It’s just the coughing and wheezing that I feel.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any changes in your bowel movements or how often you go to the bathroom?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t experienced any changes in my bowel movements or how often I go to the bathroom. Everything seems normal in that regard.

doctor_turn: Have you missed school because of your coughing fits or breathing issues?
patient_turn: Yes, I’ve missed school a few times because of the coughing fits and breathing issues. It’s been tough to keep up with everything.

doctor_turn: Has your ability to play sports or be active changed because of your coughing and breathing issues?
patient_turn: Yes, my ability to play sports has definitely changed. I’ve had to reduce how much I participate in soccer because of the coughing and wheezing. It’s frustrating!

doctor_turn: Have you had any health issues or medical problems in the past?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any health issues or medical problems in the past. I’ve been pretty healthy overall, except for some childhood rashes that were treated and mostly resolved.

doctor_turn: Does anyone in your family have asthma or allergies?
patient_turn: Yes, my sister has asthma and allergic rhinitis. She uses a daily inhaler for it.

doctor_turn: Have you ever used an inhaler or any medication for your breathing issues?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t used an inhaler or any medication for my breathing issues. I’ve just been dealing with the coughing fits and wheezing on my own.

doctor_turn: Do you have any known allergies to medications?
patient_turn: No, I don’t have any known allergies to medications. I haven’t had any issues with that.

doctor_turn: Do you often have symptoms like a runny nose or itchy eyes?
patient_turn: I occasionally have symptoms like a runny nose and itchy eyes, especially with environmental allergies. It doesn’t happen all the time, but it does happen sometimes.
